claims.author_quote: The 80-and-over population in the US is expected to grow by more than 56% over the next decade, dwarfing total population growth of around 5%. One million additional 80-plus households will materialize this year alone, doubling to two million by 2029.
claims.restatement: US senior-housing demand is outrunning supply: the 80+ cohort is inflecting sharply upward while occupancy has risen for 19 consecutive quarters and new construction sits at 2012 lows. (Author words relayed by Business Insider's same-day coverage, supplement_1 - the post itself is paywalled past the intro.)

evidence.quote: Senior housing occupancy gained 0.4 percentage points to 89.5% in the first quarter of 2026, from 89.1% in the last quarter of 2025, according to the National Investment Center for Seniors Housing & Care (NIC) using newly released data from NIC MAP. This is the 19th consecutive quarter of increasing occupancy rates.

At the same time, new units under construction fell to their lowest level since 2012, and year-over-year inventory growth hit a record low of 0.4% in the first quarter of 2026, suggesting new supply is unlikely to accelerate in the near term.

claims.restatement: The source's named senior-living picks are Welltower, Janus Living and Brookdale (relayed by Business Insider's same-day coverage of the paid note - see evidence and supplement_1). This run expresses the theme via WELL, the largest and most liquid named pick, which is currently delivering: SHO same-store NOI +22.1% YoY, occupancy +370bps, and a raised FY26 normalized-FFO guide.

evidence.quote: Citrini's analysts see a key opportunity for investors to benefit from this trend, specifically through senior living facility stocks and real estate investment trusts. It named REITS Welltower and Janus Living as two of its top senior living picks, with retirement home operator Brookdale Senior Living as the third.
Reported total portfolio year-over-year same store NOI ("SSNOI") growth of 16.4%, driven by SSNOI growth in our Seniors Housing Operating ("SHO") portfolio of 22.1%

SHO portfolio organic same store revenue growth increased 9.5% year-over-year in the first quarter, resulting from 370 basis points ("bps") of average occupancy growth and 5.0% growth in Revenue Per Occupied Room ("RevPOR")

claims.author_quote: One million additional 80-plus households will materialize this year alone, doubling to two million by 2029.
claims.restatement: The demand wave has visible near-term markers: NIC projects sector occupancy crossing 90% before end-2026, and the first boomers turn 80 in 2026.

evidence.quote: At the current pace of development and demand, NIC analysts believe senior housing occupancy is on track to surpass 90% occupancy well before the end of the year, further tightening availability across many markets.
As the nearly 70 million Baby Boomers begin turning 80 in 2026, we expect durable demand for our attractive senior housing communities located in favorable markets to increase

claims.author_quote: The AI trade is - at a minimum - crowded, even where it is right. We think the risk of "AI exhaustion" is high and it's probable that we will see some degree of rotation into things that people have seemingly stopped caring about.
claims.restatement: Capital rotation out of crowded AI into under-owned themes is imminent enough to trade. No primary source can verify positioning/attention claims; flagged insufficient rather than assumed. Note the tension the evidence exposes: WELL itself is a consensus momentum leader (52-week high, 8 sell-side target raises since Q1, consensus target only ~1% above spot), so 'under-owned' plausibly describes the smaller named picks (Brookdale, Janus Living), not WELL.

priced_in.expectations_summary: Deterministic reverse-DCF (cli imply, code-computed multiple, verbatim): 'implied FCF CAGR +10.0% (price=236.06, shares=705914450.0, net_debt=0.0, fcf=4547000000.0, exit_multiple=36.65, years=5, discount=0.1)' with the tool's own caveat that today's 36.65x multiple makes the answer the discount rate by construction. Read: at an UNCHANGED ~37x multiple on guidance-low normalized FFO, the price only earns 10%/yr if FFO compounds ~10%/yr for five years. Growth is currently clearing that hurdle (Q1 normalized FFO +23%, SHO SSNOI guided +16.5-21.5%) - so the risk is the multiple, not this year's growth: ~37x NFFO is roughly double the healthcare-REIT norm, the stock closed at its exact 52-week high (236.06, range 148.97-236.06), RSI14 79.1, +20.3% above the 200DMA, and the consensus price target (238) sits 0.8% above spot. Priced for continuation of a record year, at a record multiple, at a record price.
priced_in.variant_view: The verified variant is DURATION: 19 straight quarters of occupancy gains, construction at 2012 lows, 0.4% record-low inventory growth, and a 56%-per-decade 80+ cohort wave imply a pricing-power runway that one-year guides understate - the thesis is real and the source's picks are now attributed, not assumed. But at 37x guidance-low FFO with zero street upside and an RSI-79 tape, the market is paying for the runway today. Right theme, fully-priced flagship, wrong week: wait for the reversion trigger, or express through the smaller named picks after their own runs of this ledger.

inputs.unit: USD share price, close 2026-07-02 (also the 52-week high; matches technicals.json)
USD FY26 normalized FFO guidance, LOW end of the raised range (Exhibit 3 total $4,547M; per-share $6.21-$6.35) - low end chosen so the expectations bar is conservative

evidence.quote: We also increased the guidance range of full year normalized FFO attributable to common stockholders to a range of $6.21 to $6.35 per diluted share from the previous range of $6.09 to $6.25.

kill_switches.condition: Re-entry trigger: WELL closes within 3% of its 50DMA (spot is +10.6% above it at run date) with RSI14 < 65 - re-run the ledger for a fresh entry decision, including the smaller named picks (BKD, JAN)
Thesis damage: Q2'26 results (release scheduled 2026-07-27 after close) show SHO SSNOI growth < 12% YoY, or FY26 normalized-FFO guidance midpoint cut below $6.28 - downgrade to pass
Thesis broken: next NIC MAP quarterly release shows sector occupancy flat/down QoQ, or YoY inventory growth >= 1.0% (supply response starting) - downgrade to pass
